message:
fix(fr-store): table-form parser uses key-boundary match, not substring (br-frankenredis-r85v)

Self-review caught a fifth real defect: the prior table-form
parser used naive find('description') / find('function_name')
which matched substrings inside VALUES, not just keys. A common
case like

  redis.register_function{function_name='describe_user',
                          callback=function() ... end}

triggered the substring match on 'description' inside
'describe_user' and ended up populating description = 'describe_user',
silently corrupting FUNCTION LIST output.

Replace with extract_table_field which:
  * Walks every occurrence of the key bytes
  * Confirms the LEFT byte is not an identifier char (so
    '_function_name' doesn't match 'function_name')
  * Confirms the RIGHT byte (after optional whitespace) is
    (so 'description' inside a value doesn't match)
  * Then extracts the quoted value

New regression test
function_load_table_form_does_not_confuse_value_substring_with_key
pins the function_name='describe_user' case.

fr-store 294 passed; fr-command 656 passed; cargo clippy --tests
-D warnings clean; cargo fmt --check clean.

Slice of br-frankenredis-r85v.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
